content:
alexcrichton opened issue #13020:

Currently this function writes a number of arguments to the stack without validation that the writes are in-bounds. While most of the time they will be it's theoretically possible to configure smaller stacks which could result in out-of-bounds writes here. One example is:

$ wasmtime wast testcase.wast -W stack-switching,function-references -Wasync-stack-size=4096 -Wmax-wasm-stack=4096
zsh: segmentation fault (core dumped)  wasmtime wast testcase.wast -W stack-switching,function-references

There's some more (maybe inaccurate) information about the bug here too.
